response: Upstairs the publisher plans a book with puzzles. Each section must have a different number of puzzles. The first section has 12 puzzles. The second section has twice as many as the first. The third section has 5 more than the second. The total number of puzzles is the sum of all sections. The first section has 12 puzzles. The second section has 24 puzzles. The third section has 29 puzzles. The total number of puzzles is 65. The first section has 12 puzzles. The second section has 24 puzzles. The third section has 29 puzzles. The total number of puzzles is 65. Any other questions?
